internship_experiences.summary: Developed reproducible data analysis workflows to identify effective cancer cell reversal patterns.
impact_points: Developed reproducible data analysis workflows to identify effective cancer cell reversal patterns.
Built end-to-end analysis pipeline (cleaning, clustering, visualization) for multi-dataset research.
Applied K-means clustering to identify stable vs. significant biological response patterns.
Improved classification efficiency by 10% through optimized workflow design.

internship_experiences.title: Data Analyst Intern
internship_experiences.summary: Developed predictive models for earthquake damage assessment using sensor data.
impact_points: Developed predictive models for earthquake damage assessment using sensor data.
Built ML model to predict post-earthquake sensor readings and detect anomalies.
Reduced manual inspection workload by 15% through automated damage assessment.
Validated model predictions against real-world inspection data for reliability.

selection_logic: Industry is selected before role so the user can ride stronger policy and market tailwinds.
Scoring combines macro attractiveness, current skill fit, entry friendliness, and visa friction.
Top industries should feed company strategy before final role positioning.

target_company_types: Healthcare analytics and data infrastructure vendors
company_selection_rules: Prioritize companies where data work influences revenue, product adoption, or operational efficiency.
Prefer teams where analytics sits close to decision-makers rather than isolated reporting functions.
Use industry tailwinds to narrow company types before narrowing exact job titles.
Favor companies with clear analyst-to-strategic-role progression and measurable first-project scope.
Add screening for international hiring maturity, remote flexibility, and globally distributed teams.
industry_analysis: Healthcare AI rewards companies that connect model performance to clinical workflow adoption.
Data access, regulatory trust, and integration depth are stronger moats than generic model novelty.
Enterprise SaaS gives broad access to product, revenue, and usage data with relatively clear experimentation loops.
AI-native workflow software creates strong openings for analytics-driven product strategy roles.
Fintech companies create strong data roles where analytics links directly to growth, fraud, and retention.
The highest leverage teams sit close to risk, monetization, and operational automation.
market_analysis: Hospitals and providers buy cautiously, so workflow ROI and trust are critical.
Platform businesses that embed into existing systems often scale better than point tools.
B2B buyers now expect AI augmentation and measurable productivity gains.
Companies with product-led data loops often develop stronger analyst and data scientist scope.
Efficiency-focused buyers reward products that reduce manual operations and trust failures.
Infrastructure and B2B fintech often provide clearer data ownership than consumer-only products.
value_chain_analysis: High-value positions sit near clinical data infrastructure, documentation workflows, and decision support.
The best operating leverage comes from turning fragmented healthcare processes into repeatable data products.
The most strategic teams sit between product usage, monetization, and go-to-market efficiency.
Observability, collaboration, and workflow software each create strong instrumentation surfaces.
Payments rails, underwriting, and finance automation are strong analytics-heavy layers.
Companies with both product telemetry and transaction data tend to create richer role scope.
competitor_map: Clinical AI platforms: Tempus, Abridge, Suki
Data and oncology infrastructure: Flatiron Health, Guardant Health
Observability and infrastructure: Datadog, New Relic
Collaboration and workflow: Notion, Asana, Airtable

why_these_companies: Tempus fits because it sits inside Healthcare AI tailwinds and gives exposure to precision medicine and clinical data products.
Abridge fits because it sits inside Healthcare AI tailwinds and gives exposure to clinical documentation and workflow ai.
Datadog fits because it sits inside Enterprise SaaS tailwinds and gives exposure to instrumentation-heavy saas with strong product analytics.
HubSpot fits because it sits inside Enterprise SaaS tailwinds and gives exposure to growth, lifecycle, and customer platform analytics.
Stripe fits because it sits inside Fintech tailwinds and gives exposure to payments infrastructure and product analytics.
Ramp fits because it sits inside Fintech tailwinds and gives exposure to finance automation and spend intelligence.

decision_principles: Role path comes after industry and company strategy so the title reflects the market context.
Bridge roles are valid when they improve industry entry speed and future option value.
Choose roles where the first year can produce visible business impact, not just technical exposure.

first_month_plan: Map the operating context of Healthcare AI and learn its core metrics.
Build stakeholder understanding around precision medicine and clinical data products.
Ship one small but visible analysis, dashboard, or workflow improvement that proves execution speed.
Translate job requirements into a 30-day proof-of-value checklist.
month_2_3_plan: Own a recurring analysis or decision-support workflow instead of one-off reporting.
Develop domain fluency so recommendations reflect industry economics, not just data outputs.
Turn one technical capability into a reusable artifact such as a dashboard, model, template, or playbook.
Strengthen cross-functional trust by connecting analysis to a business decision or operational win.
one_year_plan: Become the go-to operator for data analyst problems.
Build a portfolio of impact stories that compound into promotion or stronger external positioning.
Expand from execution into prioritization by influencing what should be measured, automated, or changed.
Create option value for the next move across stronger roles, better companies, or deeper domain specialization.
daily_skill_accumulation: Spend 30-45 minutes strengthening one role-critical skill with a concrete artifact every day.
Maintain a running evidence log of business impact, stakeholder wins, and domain insights.
Read one company, industry, or market note daily to stay macro-aware instead of tool-focused.
Refine one resume or narrative bullet each week using fresh evidence from projects.
value_creation_plan: Target value creation where Healthcare AI has active tailwinds.
Focus on problems close to precision medicine and clinical data products.
Prefer work that reduces decision latency, improves forecast quality, or creates reusable operational leverage.
Document outcomes in a way that can later power interviews, resumes, and promotion cases.
cover_letter_growth_narrative: I am targeting Healthcare AI because the macro and policy context supports durable demand.
I am especially interested in Tempus because they sit close to strategic operating problems.
My role path centers on becoming a high-leverage Data Analyst who can translate analysis into decisions.
I want my first year to compound into both immediate contribution and long-term industry positioning.
